title: Introduction
description: NOC 63100 – Insurance agents and brokers | GTR Immigration Insurance agents and brokers under NOC 63100 are the risk management experts of Canada’s financial sector. Tasked with protecting the assets of individuals and businesses, these TEER 2 professionals navigate complex policy landscapes to provide essential security. As Canada’s economy grows, the demand for specialized insurance expertise in property, casualty, and life insurance is surging. For immigration, this role offers a stable and high-earning pathway to Permanent Residency (PR) through Express Entry and various Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group63 – Occupations in personal serviceSub-Major Group631 – Insurance, real estate and financial sales occupationsMinor Group6310 – Insurance, real estate and financial sales occupationsNOC Code63100

duties: Sell life, automobile, property, health, or other types of insurance to new and existing clients.
Establish client insurance coverage, calculate premiums, and establish methods of payment.
Provide information about group and individual insurance packages, the range of risk coverage, benefits, and other policy features.
Monitor insurance claims and respond to client inquiries.
Identify and solicit potential clients and build a network of leads.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Licensure by the insurance regulatory authority in the province or territory of practice is mandatory.
On-the-job training and completion of specialized insurance courses are usually required.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Employment in this sector is expected to grow alongside the population and the increasing complexity of financial risks.
labour_market_demand: The demand for insurance agents is rated as Good across most provinces, particularly in Ontario and Alberta. As the insurance industry undergoes digital transformation, there is a specific need for brokers who can combine traditional relationship-building with modern InsurTech tools. Retirements in this sector are also creating significant openings for international talent.
High Commission Potential: Unlimited earning potential through performance-based structures.
Professional Growth: Clear pathways to becoming an Agency Manager or specialized Risk Consultant.
Industry Stability: Insurance remains a mandatory and essential service in Canada.
Work-Life Balance: Many brokerage roles offer flexible schedules and hybrid work options.
pathways: Federal Skilled Worker (Express Entry): Direct eligibility for TEER 2 professionals.
OINP Human Capital Priorities: Targeted draws for financial and sales professionals.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): The fastest route for those with 1 year of Canadian experience.
